children.heading: Qualified property or services
children.label: 4 Qualified property or services
children.text: The term “qualified property or services” means any property (other than real property and other than personal property of a kind held for investment) or services which are offered for sale to customers in the ordinary course of the line of business of the employer in which the employee is performing services.

children.heading: Working condition fringe defined
children.label: d Working condition fringe defined
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “working condition fringe” means any property or services provided to an employee of the employer to the extent that, if the employee paid for such property or services, such payment would be allowable as a deduction under section 162 or 167.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The term “de minimis fringe” means any property or service the value of which is (after taking into account the frequency with which similar fringes are provided by the employer to the employer’s employees) so small as to make accounting for it unreasonable or administratively impracticable.

children.heading: Cash reimbursements
children.label: 3 Cash reimbursements
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “qualified transportation fringe” includes a cash reimbursement by an employer to an employee for a benefit described in paragraph (1). The preceding sentence shall apply to a cash reimbursement for any transit pass only if a voucher or similar item which may be exchanged only for a transit pass is not readily available for direct distribution by the employer to the employee.

children.heading: No constructive receipt
children.label: 4 No constructive receipt
children.text: No amount shall be included in the gross income of an employee solely because the employee may choose between any qualified transportation fringe and compensation which would otherwise be includible in gross income of such employee.

children.heading: Qualified parking
children.label: C Qualified parking
children.text: The term “qualified parking” means parking provided to an employee on or near the business premises of the employer or on or near a location from which the employee commutes to work by transportation described in subparagraph (A), in a commuter highway vehicle, or by carpool. Such term shall not include any parking on or near property used by the employee for residential purposes.

children.heading: Transportation provided by employer
children.label: D Transportation provided by employer
children.text: Transportation referred to in paragraph (1)(A) shall be considered to be provided by an employer if such transportation is furnished in a commuter highway vehicle operated by or for the employer.

children.heading: Employee
children.label: E Employee
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “employee” does not include an individual who is an employee within the meaning of section 401(c)(1).

children.heading: Rounding
children.label: B Rounding
children.text: If any increase determined under subparagraph (A) is not a multiple of $5, such increase shall be rounded to the next lowest multiple of $5.

children.heading: Coordination with other provisions
children.label: 7 Coordination with other provisions
children.text: For purposes of this section, the terms “working condition fringe” and “de minimis fringe” shall not include any qualified transportation fringe (determined without regard to paragraph (2)).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The term “qualified moving expense reimbursement” means any amount received (directly or indirectly) by an individual from an employer as a payment for (or a reimbursement of) expenses which would be deductible as moving expenses under section 217 if directly paid or incurred by the individual. Such term shall not include any payment for (or reimbursement of) an expense actually deducted by the individual in a prior taxable year.

children.label: 3 Special rule for parents in the case of air transportation
children.text: Any use of air transportation by a parent of an employee (determined without regard to paragraph (1)(B)) shall be treated as use by the employee.

children.heading: Exclusions under subsection (a)(1) and (2) apply to highly compensated employees only if no discrimination
children.label: 1 Exclusions under subsection (a)(1) and (2) apply to highly compensated employees only if no discrimination
children.text: Paragraphs (1) and (2) of subsection (a) shall apply with respect to any fringe benefit described therein provided with respect to any highly compensated employee only if such fringe benefit is available on substantially the same terms to each member of a group of employees which is defined under a reasonable classification set up by the employer which does not discriminate in favor of highly compensated employees.

children.heading: Leased section of department store
children.label: B Leased section of department store
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), a leased section of a department store is any part of a department store where over-the-counter sales of property are made under a lease or similar arrangement where it appears to the general public that individuals making such sales are employed by the person operating the department store.

children.label: B Qualified affiliate
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “qualified affiliate” means any corporation which is predominantly engaged in airline-related services.

children.label: D Affiliated group
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “affiliated group” has the meaning given such term by section 1504(a).

children.heading: Highly compensated employee
children.label: 6 Highly compensated employee
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “highly compensated employee” has the meaning given such term by section 414(q).

children.heading: Air cargo
children.label: 7 Air cargo
children.text: For purposes of subsection (b), the transportation of cargo by air and the transportation of passengers by air shall be treated as the same service.

children.heading: Application of section to otherwise taxable educational or training benefits
children.label: 8 Application of section to otherwise taxable educational or training benefits
children.text: Amounts paid or expenses incurred by the employer for education or training provided to the employee which are not excludable from gross income under section 127 shall be excluded from gross income under this section if (and only if) such amounts or expenses are a working condition fringe.

children.heading: Customers not to include employees
children.label: k Customers not to include employees
children.text: For purposes of this section (other than subsection (c)(2)), the term “customers” shall only include customers who are not employees.

children.heading: Section not to apply to fringe benefits expressly provided for elsewhere
children.label: l Section not to apply to fringe benefits expressly provided for elsewhere
children.text: This section (other than subsections (e) and (g)) shall not apply to any fringe benefits of a type the tax treatment of which is expressly provided for in any other section of this chapter.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “qualified retirement planning services” means any retirement planning advice or information provided to an employee and his spouse by an employer maintaining a qualified employer plan.

children.heading: Nondiscrimination rule
children.label: 2 Nondiscrimination rule
children.text: Subsection (a)(7) shall apply in the case of highly compensated employees only if such services are available on substantially the same terms to each member of the group of employees normally provided education and information regarding the employer’s qualified employer plan.

children.heading: Qualified employer plan
children.label: 3 Qualified employer plan
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “qualified employer plan” means a plan, contract, pension, or account described in section 219(g)(5).

children.heading: Qualified military base realignment and closure fringe
children.label: n Qualified military base realignment and closure fringe

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The term “qualified military base realignment and closure fringe” means 1 or more payments under the authority of section 1013 of the Demonstration Cities and Metropolitan Development Act of 1966 () (as in effect on the date of the enactment of the American Recovery and Reinvestment Tax Act of 2009).42 U.S.C. 3374

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 2 Limitation
children.text: With respect to any property, such term shall not include any payment referred to in paragraph (1) to the extent that the sum of all of such payments related to such property exceeds the maximum amount described in subsection (c) of such section (as in effect on such date).

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: o Regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as may be necessary or appropriate to carry out the purposes of this section.
